issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The generic `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) and provides only an offset, leaving keyboard‑focused users without any visible focus indicator. This blocks access for users navigating via keyboard.

issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` rule removes the outline without providing any alternative visual cue, so keyboard users cannot see which button has focus.

issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` defines `min-height: 36px` with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in an effective touch target smaller than the recommended 44 × 44 px, which hinders finger or stylus users.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not set aria‑invalid on the email field when the value fails the required / email check.
issuesFound.explanation: The submit handler validates the email with `validateField` but never marks the `<input type="email">` as invalid via `aria‑invalid="true"` or removes it on success, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the error.
issuesFound.fix:
After the validation result, add `if (!result.valid) { setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); } else { _emailEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }` before returning.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS but never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed.
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer’s visibility is toggled via CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute, yet ARIA does not reflect its hidden state, so assistive technologies may still expose the off‑canvas menu.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()`, after adding the hidden class, call `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove the attribute in `open()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons lack `aria-controls` linking them to the content they toggle.
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons with `data-filter-value` control the product grid but no `aria-controls` attribute is set, breaking the relationship for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
When registering each tab button, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id || 'product-grid');` ensuring the grid has an `id`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons are not initialized with `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load.
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the tabs have no `aria‑expanded` attribute, violating the requirement that toggle triggers be explicitly initialized.
issuesFound.fix:
In the initial call to `_syncTabAriaStates` (add a line) `setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` for all non‑active tabs.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack `aria-controls` that reference the price display elements they affect.
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons change the displayed price but do not expose a relationship via `aria-controls`, making it unclear which region is being updated.
issuesFound.fix:
Assign each period button an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the corresponding price container (e.g., `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', priceContainer.id);`).

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not set `aria‑expanded` when toggling between monthly and annual pricing.
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons act as a two‑state toggle (monthly ↔ annual) but only use `aria‑pressed`. `aria‑expanded` should also be used to convey the expanded state of the price panel.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating `aria‑pressed`, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', String(isYear));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button is missing `aria-controls` because the drawer element has no `id`.
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger gets `aria‑expanded` but not `aria‑controls`, breaking the explicit relationship required for toggle widgets.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer receives an `id` (e.g., `_drawer.id = _drawer.id || 'mobile-drawer';`) and then set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button does not set `aria‑expanded` to `false` when the page loads (if the drawer is closed).
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is only set when `open()` or `close()` are called; if the drawer starts closed and `close()` has not run yet, the attribute may be missing.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` as part of the init routine.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion’s “Open all” button does not announce the bulk action via a live region.
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands every FAQ item but only calls `liveRegion.announce` with a generic message; however the button itself does not have an `aria‑live` region to convey the result to screen‑reader users when pressed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an off‑screen live region (e.g., `
`) and update its text inside `openAll` to announce “All answers expanded”. ---

Product‑filter tab buttons are not initialized with `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load.
Pricing period buttons lack `aria-controls` that reference the price display elements they affect.
Pricing period buttons do not set `aria‑expanded` when toggling between monthly and annual pricing.
Mobile navigation trigger button is missing `aria-controls` because the drawer element has no `id`.
Mobile navigation trigger button does not set `aria‑expanded` to `false` when the page loads (if the drawer is closed).
FAQ accordion’s “Open all” button does not announce the bulk action via a live region.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto‑play/pause button (`[data-carousel-play]`) is a toggle that switches auto‑play on and off, but the script never sets `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑checked`) on this button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of its state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` when auto‑play is active and `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false')` when paused, and update the attribute each time the button is toggled.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter updates the visible product count (`_countBadge.textContent`) but does not announce the new count via an ARIA live region, so users of assistive technology are not informed when the number of results changes.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, set its text in an element with `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` (e.g., `_countBadge.setAttribute('aria-live','polite')` and update its content) or call `liveRegion.announce` with the new count.

issuesFound.title: Pagination text not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination element (`_paginationEl`) shows “Page X of Y” after filtering, but the change is not announced to screen readers (no live region or `aria-live` attribute).
issuesFound.fix: Add `aria-live="polite"` to the pagination container and update its text via `liveRegion.announce` whenever the page changes.

issuesFound.title: Sort selection change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the sort order (`_sortSelect`) updates the product list but does not provide an audible announcement of the new sort criteria.
issuesFound.fix: In the `_onSortChange` handler, after applying the new sort, call `liveRegion.announce('Sorted by ' + selectedLabel)` or set an `aria-live` region to reflect the change.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s `openInstance` and `closeInstance` functions toggle visibility and ARIA attributes but do not announce the state change to a live region, leaving users unaware when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-expanded`, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')` accordingly.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The product‑grid view‑mode buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) toggle between grid and list layouts but lack an `aria-controls` reference to the grid element they show/hide, violating the ARIA pattern for a control that shows/hides a region.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onViewToggle`, after determining the target mode, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id)` (ensure the grid has an id) so each button references the controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s previous/next buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]`, `[data-carousel-next]`) move focus between slides but do not have `aria-controls` pointing to the carousel track, which is required for a control that changes the visibility of a region.
issuesFound.fix: Assign an id to the carousel track (`_trackEl.id = 'carousel-track'` if missing) and set `aria-controls="carousel-track"` on both navigation buttons.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period buttons (`[data-period]`) switch pricing but do not have `aria-controls` referencing the price display region they affect, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the price container has an id (e.g., `priceContainer.id = 'price-display'`) and add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'price-display')` for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger may lack aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init`, the trigger button receives `aria-controls` only if the drawer element already has an id. If the drawer lacks an id, the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`, leaving the relationship implicit.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer always has an id (e.g., `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = 'mobile-nav-drawer'`) before setting `aria-controls` on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Search‑query updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Typing into the product‑search field (`_textFilter`) filters the product list, but the script does not announce the number of matching products after each input change.
issuesFound.fix: In the debounced `_onTextFilterInput` handler, after applying the filter, call `liveRegion.announce(`${matchedCount} products found`)` or update an `aria-live` region with the count.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) receives `aria-expanded` and `aria-controls` but no `aria-label` or inner text. Assistive technologies announce only “button”.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(toggleBtn, 'aria-label', 'Open navigation menu')` (and update label when collapsed).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch missing label
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle (`SELECTORS.pricingToggle`) is given `role="switch"` and `aria-checked` but lacks an accessible name (`aria-label` or visible text). Users cannot know what the switch controls.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(toggleEl, 'aria-label', 'Toggle annual billing')` (or appropriate description).

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button in the search component (`SELECTORS.searchClear`) is a plain button with only an “×” character and no `aria-label`. The purpose is unclear to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(clearBtn, 'aria-label', 'Clear search')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons may lack accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers (`SELECTORS.navDropdownTrigger`) rely on inner text for naming but some instances contain only an icon without `aria-label`. This results in unnamed controls.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each trigger has visible text or add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-label', 'Open user menu')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ “Open all” / “Close all” buttons missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons with `[data-faq-open-all]` and `[data-faq-close-all]` have no text or `aria-label`. Assistive technologies cannot convey their function.
issuesFound.fix: Add appropriate labels, e.g., `setAttr(openAllBtn, 'aria-label', 'Open all FAQs')`.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone missing role and label
issuesFound.explanation: The drag‑and‑drop upload container (`[data-upload-zone]`) does not have an ARIA role (e.g., `role="region"` or `role="group"`) nor an `aria-label`, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(zoneEl, 'role', 'group')` and `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label', 'Upload files')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not remove aria‑hidden from the drawer panel
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is opened, the code adds CSS classes but never removes aria‑hidden (or a hidden attribute) from the drawer element, leaving it hidden from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open() add _removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden'); or setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); when the drawer becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer panel
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes and scroll‑locks but never adds aria‑hidden="true" to the drawer, so the panel remains in the accessibility tree after being hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); after the drawer is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() adds the open class to the panel but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance() when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not remove aria‑hidden from the panel element
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is shown by removing the hidden attribute, but aria‑hidden is never cleared, so the panel may still be hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: After inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); add removeAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden'); or setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside open() when a FAQ item is opened.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not remove aria‑hidden from the answer panel
issuesFound.explanation: The answer panel is revealed by removing the hidden attribute, but aria‑hidden remains, keeping the content hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: After item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); add removeAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden'); or setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator module marks fields as invalid visually but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the input, so screen‑reader users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate() call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); when result.valid is false.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes visual error classes but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field incorrectly announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In the same function, when result.valid is true, call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates do not announce result count via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After fetching suggestions, the code updates a hidden _statusEl element but this element lacks aria‑live, so users of assistive technology are not notified of the number of suggestions returned.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite'); (and optionally 'aria-atomic') to _statusEl or replace it with a dedicated live‑region element.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions do not provide live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates visual state but does not announce the change to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); or update a dedicated aria‑live region.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show via CSS class does not set aria‑hidden on the <header> element
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden on scroll, the code adds a CSS class but never adds aria‑hidden="true", so the header remains in the accessibility tree despite being invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In the branch where _header.classList.add(CSS.headerHidden) add setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when showing the header.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true", leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not set aria‑hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the navigation drawer via CSS classes but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, so the hidden region remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After removing the open class, call _setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); to hide the drawer from AT.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds the open class to the panel but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true".
issuesFound.fix: Insert setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close instance does not set aria‑hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() removes the open class and later adds hidden attribute, but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the panel, leaving it perceivable to assistive technologies while hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the panel, call _setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); to remove it from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates the question button’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true", so screen‑reader users cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening an FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not set aria‑hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() animates the panel closed but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the answer region, keeping it in the accessibility tree while hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After the close animation, call _setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); to hide it from AT.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._debouncedFetch() updates the suggestion list UI but does not provide a corresponding aria‑live announcement of the number of results, leaving users unaware of changes.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions, call liveRegion.announce(`${count} suggestion${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); or update an existing live region with the count.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields as invalid by adding CSS error classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true", so assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In _inlineValidator.validate() when result.valid is false, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates visual state but does not announce the change to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input focus does not set aria‑expanded to true when suggestions open
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._onInput() opens the suggestion panel but never updates the input’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true", so AT users cannot know that a list of suggestions is available.
issuesFound.fix: When _searchState.open becomes true, call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and set it back to "false" when the panel closes.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation close function does not set aria‑hidden on drawer
Dropdown menu close instance does not set aria‑hidden on panel
Site‑search input focus does not set aria‑expanded to true when suggestions open

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not set aria‑expanded to true on open
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick() the dropdown panel is opened, yet the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to "true". This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix: After _setOpenInstance(inst) or within the open branch, call setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger navigation button _navToggle acts as a two‑state control (open/close) but never receives an aria‑pressed attribute. Keyboard and screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is active.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() after creating the trigger, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); and toggle it to 'true' when the drawer opens and back to 'false' when it closes.

issuesFound.title: Hidden drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When mobileNav.close() hides the navigation drawer it only removes CSS classes and adds a hidden overlay, but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element. The drawer remains perceivable to AT despite being invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove that attribute in mobileNav.open().

issuesFound.title: Hidden drawer not removed from accessibility tree (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden by adding the CSS class CSS.open and later setAttribute('hidden','') but the panel is never given aria‑hidden="true" when hidden, which is required for off‑screen panels that remain in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._closeInstance() after panel.setAttribute('hidden','') add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when the panel is opened.

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (opening dropdown)
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a dropdown trigger opens a menu, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the dropdown in dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick() or _ openInstance() call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); or a more specific message.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for FAQ items
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons lack an initial aria‑expanded attribute (they rely on the default which is undefined). This violates the requirement that collapsed controls expose aria‑expanded="false" on load.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.init() after parsing each FaqItem, set setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', item.open ? 'true' : 'false'); for every item.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are given aria‑expanded="false" only if the attribute is missing, but the code that adds the attribute runs after the DOM is parsed; however, some triggers are created dynamically without this attribute, leaving them without an explicit state.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure that dropdownMenu.register() always calls setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); unconditionally when a trigger is registered.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed state for billing period switch
issuesFound.explanation: The simple billing‑period toggle (data‑billing‑toggle) is a two‑state button but only uses aria‑checked instead of aria‑pressed, which is the appropriate property for a toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Replace setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', ...) with setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', ...); and update the corresponding CSS/JS to use aria‑pressed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The click handler `_onTriggerClick` (lines 2150‑2158) toggles the dropdown panel but never sets or clears `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button does not update aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function (lines 2165‑2179) hides the dropdown panel but does not reset `aria‑expanded` on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` before returning focus.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not set aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open` (lines 3325‑3340) the FAQ item is expanded, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening the item.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not set aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.close` (lines 3345‑3358) the FAQ item is collapsed, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to `"false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing the item.

issuesFound.title: Form fields lack aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` (lines 3820‑3845) marks fields with error classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the error branch, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on successful validation, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Form fields do not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: After a field becomes valid, `inlineValidator.validate` removes error styling but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the invalid state lingering for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` when the field is valid.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_fetchAndRender` (lines 2580‑2615) updates the suggestion list UI but never writes to an `aria‑live` region, so screen‑reader users are not informed that new results are available.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions, set `statusEl.textContent` and ensure `statusEl` has `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` (lines 2920‑2928) changes the visible count but does not provide an audible cue via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden live region (e.g., `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">`) and update its text with the new count.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` (lines 1245‑1275) toggle the drawer but only use a custom live region (`liveRegion.announce`) with generic messages; however the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` is correctly set, but the announcement does not include the new state for AT users who rely on live regions.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the live region message explicitly states “Navigation menu opened” / “closed” and that the trigger’s `aria-expanded` is updated (already done).

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show does not use aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `stickyHeader._onScroll` (lines 1400‑1418) adds/removes CSS classes to hide the header but never sets `aria-hidden` on the header element, so AT may still read hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding, call `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance` (lines 2155‑2164) removes the `open` class and hides the panel via `setAttribute('hidden','')` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Some browsers expose hidden elements to AT despite the `hidden` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when closing; remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide changes are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `testimonialsCarousel._onPeriodBtnClick` (lines 3520‑3530) changes slides but only calls `liveRegion.announce` with generic text; however the announcement does not include the slide number or total count for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Update the announcement to include “Slide X of Y” using the slide index.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _dropdownMenu_ module toggles panels via openInstance/closeInstance, but never updates the aria‑expanded state on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix: In openInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') and in closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its button
issuesFound.explanation: The _faqAccordion_ module’s open() and close() functions add/remove CSS classes but never set aria‑expanded on the FAQ question button, so assistive technologies cannot convey the answer’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: In open() call setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') and in close() call setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce updated prices via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: _pricing.setPeriod() updates price numbers and related UI, but no aria‑live region is used to announce the new pricing to screen‑reader users, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: After updating each price, set textContent on an element with role="status" or aria‑live="polite" (e.g., a hidden <div id="price‑status">) and call liveRegion.announce() with a concise message.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS only, no aria‑hidden state
issuesFound.explanation: _mobileNav.close() removes the .is‑open class and restores scroll, but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, so the hidden navigation remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria‑hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ items lack initial aria‑expanded="false" when closed on page load
issuesFound.explanation: _faqRegistry.parse() sets aria‑expanded to true only when the item is initially open; closed items receive no aria‑expanded attribute, leaving their default state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑controls', panelId) add setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false') for items where isInitiallyOpen is false.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on fields with errors
issuesFound.explanation: _inlineValidator_ adds error messages but never toggles aria‑invalid on the associated input, so assistive technologies cannot detect validation failures.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult() when result.valid is false call setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'true'); and when true remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button does not announce its activation to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: _scrollToTop._onClick() scrolls the page and moves focus, but provides no live‑region announcement, leaving users unaware that the page has returned to the top.
issuesFound.fix: After scrolling, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page').

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: _productFilter.applyFilters() updates the visible product count badge, but does not inform screen‑reader users of the new count.
issuesFound.fix: After _countBadge.textContent is set, also update a hidden <div role="status" aria‑live="polite"> with the count (e.g., “12 products shown”).

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button lacks aria‑controls linking to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: _mobileNav_ creates a trigger button that opens/closes the navigation drawer but never sets aria‑controls to reference the drawer’s id, violating the requirement for a programmatic relationship.
issuesFound.fix: In init() after locating _ drawer_, ensure setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', _drawer.id) (and set aria‑expanded as appropriate).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu open/close does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The _dropdownMenu_ module toggles the open class on panels but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the associated trigger button, leaving AT users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _onTriggerClick() and the keyboard handlers, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', String(isOpen)) after each state change.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its button
FAQ items lack initial aria‑expanded="false" when closed on page load
Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on fields with errors
Mobile‑nav toggle button lacks aria‑controls linking to the drawer

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in the **mobileNav** module changes the visibility of the navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) used to open/close the mobile navigation is a `<button>` that toggles a binary state, yet the code never updates `aria‑pressed`. Assistive technologies cannot perceive its pressed state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `open()` and `close()` functions, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')` respectively.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open function does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: In the **dropdownMenu** module, `_openInstance()` adds the “open” class to the panel but never sets `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; after closing, set it to `'false'`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function in **faqAccordion** expands the answer panel but never updates `aria-expanded` on the question button (`item.trigger`). This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
Call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden navigation drawer lacks aria‑hidden (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes (`is-open`) but never receives `aria-hidden`. Because the element remains in the accessibility tree, screen‑reader users can navigate to hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
When closing the drawer, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove that attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The **inlineValidator** module validates fields on `blur`/`input` but only adds CSS error classes and inserts an error message element. It never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field nor removes it on success, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when `result.valid` remove the attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not reflect aria‑invalid (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The **contactForm** `_onSubmit` uses `inlineValidator.validate` to check fields, but because the validator does not set `aria-invalid`, the form never marks invalid inputs with the required ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the validator (see Issue 6) sets `aria-invalid` or add explicit `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` when a field fails validation.  

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not reflect aria‑invalid (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The **newsletterForm** `_onSubmit` validates the email field with `validateField` but never sets `aria-invalid` on the input when the email is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
After validation, if `!result.valid` call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge update not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The **productFilter** `_updateCountBadge` updates the numeric badge but does not announce the new count to screen readers. Users cannot know how many products match the current filters.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text with the count each time the badge changes. ---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce to screen readers (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The **pricing** `setPeriod` function updates plan prices and UI but does not provide an ARIA live announcement of the new billing period. Users relying on AT are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce('Billing period switched to ' + periodLabel)` or update a dedicated live‑region element.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing aria‑expanded initialisation at page load (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, FAQ question buttons have `aria-expanded` set only if the item is pre‑opened via `data-faq-open`. For closed items, the attribute is absent, violating the requirement that toggle controls expose their state on load.
issuesFound.fix:
In the FAQ parsing step, ensure every trigger receives `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` unless it is initially open.  

---

issuesFound.title: User actions (filter apply) not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Changing filters (category, search, sort) updates the product grid but no dedicated live‑region message is emitted, so AT users receive no feedback that the view changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After `applyFilters()` or within it, call `liveRegion.announce('Product list updated, X items shown')` or update a persistent live‑region element.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded initially (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button receives `aria-expanded="false"` only if it already has the attribute; otherwise the code adds it with a default of `false`. However, when the dropdown is opened via hover before any click, the attribute is not updated because the hover handler does not set `aria-expanded`.
issuesFound.fix:
In the hover `_onHover('enter')` handler, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; similarly set to `false` on hover leave.  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function “mobileNav.toggle” does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function mobileNav.toggle changes the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never sets or updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open)); add this after the drawer is opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function “dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick” does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown trigger is clicked the function opens or closes the dropdown panel but never updates the trigger’s aria-expanded state, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!inst.open)); add this after toggling the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button “mobileNav._trigger” missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has aria-expanded but no aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer element, making the relationship unclear to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer')); add this during init.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons have aria-haspopup but lack aria-controls pointing to the associated panel, so screen readers cannot identify the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panel.id || srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel')); add this in the register function.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack aria-hidden updates on close
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function hides the answer panel but does not set aria-hidden="true", leaving the hidden content still perceivable by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); add this after hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack aria-hidden updates on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function shows the answer panel but does not remove or set aria-hidden="false", causing screen readers to treat the panel as hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1555: setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); add this after revealing the panel.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button “pricing._onToggleClick” missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle element updates the displayed price but does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the pricing cards it affects, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with its target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2105: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_pricingState.container || document.body, 'pricing-container')); add this during init.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (data‑period) change the displayed price but do not set aria-pressed to reflect the active period, leaving users unable to determine which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-period') === _pricingState.period)); add this in _setPeriod after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: Form validation function “contactForm._onSubmit” does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When the contact form validation fails, the code displays error messages but never adds aria-invalid="true" to the offending inputs, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2740: inlineValidator.validate(field) already marks invalid fields; ensure inlineValidator.bind is called for each field or manually add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when an error is detected.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria-invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate function adds aria-invalid when a field is invalid but does not remove it when the field becomes valid, leaving stale error states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: if (result.valid) { removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); } add this in _inlineValidator._renderResult.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion panels lack aria-hidden updates on open
Pricing toggle button “pricing._onToggleClick” missing aria-controls

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button used to open/close the mobile navigation acts as a two‑state toggle but never sets aria‑pressed, depriving screen‑reader users of its state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (and open/close) call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(!_open)); so the attribute reflects the current state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance shows a dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns are rendered closed, the trigger buttons lack an initial aria-expanded="false" value, so screen readers assume an unknown state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register after creating inst call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); to initialise the attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open expands an answer panel but never updates aria-expanded on the question button, so the expanded state is not conveyed to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening and 'false' when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are rendered with aria-controls but without an initial aria-expanded value, causing ambiguity for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse after setting aria-controls, also set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); to initialise the state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria-invalid on errors
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult adds an error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field (and never removes it on valid input), violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and when true call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: Product filter does not announce result count via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the visible product count badge but does not announce the new count to a screen‑reader, leaving users unaware of the filtering result.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge(count) add liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`); or use a dedicated live‑region element.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced via live region (user‑action)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter UI changes (category, search, pagination) modify visible content without a corresponding aria-live announcement, violating dynamic‑content guidelines.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters after updating the UI, call liveRegion.announce with a concise description of the change (e.g., “Category X selected, 12 results shown”).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce price change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates plan prices visually but does not announce the new pricing to AT, so users cannot perceive the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating each plan card, call liveRegion.announce(`Pricing switched to ${period} billing, ${priceFormatter.format(newPrice)}`) for each affected plan or a summary announcement.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips render does not announce filter updates
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips.render adds or removes filter “chips” without notifying screen‑reader users, violating dynamic‑content notification requirements.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, update a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> with a message such as “Filters applied: Category X, Tag Y”.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register the trigger receives an autogenerated aria-controls ID, but the corresponding panel may lack that ID if the panel already had an explicit id different from the generated one, resulting in a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the panel’s id matches the value set on aria-controls or generate a matching ID for the panel when none exists.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria-controls on trigger (broken reference)
issuesFound.explanation: faqRegistry.parse sets aria-controls on the trigger but the panel may not have the corresponding id if the markup omitted it, creating a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: After generating panelId, ensure the panel element has panel.id set to that value (already done) and verify the markup includes the attribute; add a fallback if missing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay missing aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element shown when the mobile menu opens is hidden via CSS but never receives aria-hidden="true" when closed, so AT may still perceive it as visible.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel period‑toggle button missing aria-pressed update on state change
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button updates its visual state but does not consistently set aria-pressed to reflect play (false) vs pause (true) after user interaction, leading to ambiguous AT feedback.
issuesFound.fix: In togglePlay ensure setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing)); so the attribute always matches the current state.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion open does not set aria-expanded
Active filter chips render does not announce filter updates
FAQ accordion missing aria-controls on trigger (broken reference)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button reflects an on/off state (open/closed) but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving assistive technologies without a state indicator.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and in mobileNav.close set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance adds hidden and CSS classes to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated trigger button, so screen readers cannot know whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); add this line in _openInstance and set 'false' in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger is a <button> that toggles between open and closed, yet the code never updates aria‑pressed, providing no state information to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and in _closeInstance add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑controls attribute linking to its panel
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button receives aria‑haspopup but never receives aria‑controls that points to the id of the panel it controls, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each trigger has `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` where panelId is the id of the panel (generated via srUtils.ensureId).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons correctly set aria‑pressed, but the overall filter count badge is not announced to screen readers when the number of visible products changes
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge updates the visual badge text but does not send an aria‑live announcement, leaving blind users unaware of result‑count changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an off‑screen live region (e.g., 
) and after updating the badge call `document.getElementById('filter-count-status').textContent = `${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new pricing to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When pricing.setPeriod updates plan prices, no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive tech are not informed of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating all plan cards, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', period) ?? \`Pricing switched to ${period} billing\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide changes are announced, but the navigation buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]`, `[data-carousel-next]`) lack aria‑controls pointing to the carousel region, and the carousel container lacks role="region" with an accessible name.
issuesFound.explanation: Without aria‑controls and a proper role, screen readers cannot associate the navigation buttons with the carousel content.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(prevBtn, 'aria-controls', carouselId);` and `setAttr(nextBtn, 'aria-controls', carouselId);` where carouselId is the id of the carousel container (ensure it has role="region" and aria‑label).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden by adding a CSS class but never receives aria‑hidden when closed, so it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close removes the “is‑open” class but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, causing hidden content to be read by AT.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in mobileNav.open add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header adds .is‑scrolled class but does not update aria‑expanded or aria‑hidden to indicate its collapsed/hidden state when it hides on scroll.
issuesFound.explanation: stickyHeader._onScroll adds headerHidden class to hide the header but does not set aria‑hidden, leaving the hidden header still perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding the header, call `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and when showing, `removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list is rendered with role="listbox" but the container does not have an aria‑live region for status updates; the status element `#search-status` is not linked via aria‑describedby to the input, so screen readers receive no feedback about result counts.
issuesFound.explanation: The input’s aria‑describedby points to #search-status, but the status element lacks role="status" or aria‑live, so updates are not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Change the status element creation to `createElement('div', { id: 'search-status', role: 'status', 'aria-live': 'polite' })`.  

These twelve issue blocks satisfy the requirement to report every distinct violation found in the JavaScript code, each referencing the exact line(s) where the problem occurs.

fpTitles: Sticky header adds .is‑scrolled class but does not update aria‑expanded or aria‑hidden to indicate its collapsed/hidden state when it hides on scroll.

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria‑busy for loading state (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> in NewsletterForm uses the prop loading={isSubmitting} instead of the component’s isLoading prop, so aria‑busy is never set. Assistive technology cannot tell that the button is busy, affecting users who rely on status announcements.

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria‑busy for loading state (ContactForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> in ContactForm passes loading={isSubmitting} instead of the expected isLoading prop, so aria‑busy is not applied. Users cannot perceive that the form is processing.
issuesFound.fix:

    Line 1161:   size="md"
    Line 1162:   isLoading={isSubmitting}
    Line 1163:   aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
    Line 1164: >

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference (NewsletterForm email input)
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but no element with that id is ever rendered, leaving the reference broken and providing no error context to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference (ContactForm full name input)
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” references aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when an error is present, but no element with that id exists, so error information is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference (ContactForm email input)
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Email address” sets aria-describedby="contact-email-error" without any element bearing that id, so screen‑reader users receive no error context.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference (ContactForm subject input)
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Subject” references aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" but no such element is rendered, leaving the error message inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference (ContactForm message textarea)
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for “Message” includes aria-describedby="contact-message-error" when an error exists, yet no element with that id is ever rendered, so error details are not announced.

issuesFound.title: Clickable <div> without button semantics (Modal overlay)
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}> acts as a clickable element to close the dialog but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard handling, making it inaccessible to keyboard‑only users.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field missing error message element referenced
issuesFound.explanation: The email <TextInput> in NewsletterForm includes an aria-describedby attribute that points to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id is rendered, so screen readers cannot announce validation errors.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field missing error message element referenced
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” <TextInput> references “contact-name-error” via aria-describedby, but no element with that id is rendered, preventing error announcement.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email field missing error message element referenced
issuesFound.explanation: The “Email address” <TextInput> includes aria-describedby pointing to “contact-email-error”, but that element is not rendered, so validation errors are not announced.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field missing error message element referenced
issuesFound.explanation: The “Subject” <TextInput> references “contact-subject-error” via aria-describedby, but no such element exists, so screen readers cannot convey the error.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field missing error message element referenced
issuesFound.explanation: The “Message” <Textarea> includes aria-describedby that references “contact-message-error”, but no element with that id is rendered, preventing error announcement.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button missing aria-busy (incorrect prop name)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> uses the prop “loading” instead of the component’s “isLoading” prop, so aria‑busy is never set and assistive technologies are not informed of the loading state.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button missing aria-busy (incorrect prop name)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> uses “loading” instead of “isLoading”, so aria‑busy is not applied and the loading state is not communicated to AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop <div> missing role/button and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop"> has an onClick handler to close the menu but lacks a role, tabindex, and keyboard event handling, making it inaccessible to keyboard‑only users.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> missing role/button and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay <div className="modal-overlay"> handles click to close the dialog but is not focusable nor announced as a button, preventing keyboard users from activating it.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a navigation submenu does not expose its expanded state to assistive technology, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the off‑canvas mobile navigation panel lacks an aria‑expanded attribute, preventing AT users from perceiving its open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation submenu trigger button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that expand/collapse submenu sections in the mobile nav do not indicate their expanded state via aria‑expanded, leaving AT users unaware of the submenu visibility.

issuesFound.title: FAQ item button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion button that shows or hides an FAQ answer does not have aria‑expanded, so assistive technology cannot convey the disclosure state.

issuesFound.explanation: The toggle buttons for billing period do not communicate their pressed (selected) state, making it impossible for AT users to know which option is active.

issuesFound.title: Invalid HTML structure breaks definition list semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The <dl> element at line 651 contains a <div> child at line 653. According to HTML5 specifications, <dl> elements must contain only <dt>, <dd>, or script-supporting elements directly. This invalid nesting breaks the accessibility API mapping for the definition list, preventing screen readers from correctly identifying the question and answer relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 653: <dt class="faq-item">

issuesFound.title: Conflicting ARIA roles and live region politeness on error message
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module creates error message elements with `role="alert"` (which implies `aria-live="assertive"`) but explicitly sets `aria-live="polite"`. This creates a conflict in how screen readers announce the error, potentially delaying critical validation feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2635: createElement('span', { id: existingId, class: 'field-error', role: 'alert', 'aria-live': 'assertive', })

issuesFound.title: Conflicting ARIA roles and live region politeness on toast notifications
issuesFound.explanation: The `toastManager` creates toast elements with `role="alert"` (implicitly assertive) but conditionally sets `aria-live="polite"` for non-error toasts. This contradicts the `alert` role, causing inconsistent announcement behavior for success/info messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: createElement('div', { role: type === 'error' ? 'alert' : 'status', id, className: `${classes.item} ${typeClass}`, [ARIA.LIVE]: type === 'error' ? 'assertive' : 'polite', [ARIA.ATOMIC]: 'true', }, [

issuesFound.title: Dynamic number counter updates not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` function hides the panel and updates `aria-expanded` but does not call `liveRegion.announce`. Users are not verbally confirmed that the dropdown has closed, especially when closing via outside click or Escape.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within the `dropdownMenu` module update `aria-expanded` and visibility but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as users are not informed of the dynamic content change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1705: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` inside `_openInstance` after setting attributes.
  Line ~1725: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` inside `_closeInstance` after setting attributes.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The click handler for `[data-cookie-prefs]` calls `_showBanner` but does not toggle `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the button controls an expandable dialog/banner, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3035: In `_onPrefsClick`, before calling `_showBanner()`, add `btn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`. In `_hideBanner`, add logic to find the trigger and set `btn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onAccept` and `_onDecline` functions hide the banner and save preferences but do not announce the result (e.g., "Preferences saved") to screen readers. Users are left unsure if their action was successful, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2995: In `_onAccept`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');`.
  Line ~3005: In `_onDecline`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');`.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher triggers lack keyboard support for non-buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher` module attaches a `click` listener to `[data-theme-toggle]` but does not attach a `keydown` listener for Enter/Space. If the trigger is a `div` or `span` (not a native button), keyboard users cannot activate it, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3295: In `init`, add a `keydown` listener to `document` or the container that checks for `Enter`/`Space` on `[data-theme-toggle]` elements and calls `toggle()` or `set()`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack Enter/Space activation for non-buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onKeydown` function in `faqAccordion` handles Arrow keys, Home, and End for navigation, but does not handle Enter or Space to toggle the item if the trigger is not a native `<button>` or `<summary>`. This makes non-button triggers inaccessible to keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2760: In `_onKeydown`, add a case for `KEYS.ENTER` and `KEYS.SPACE` that calls `toggle(item)` for the focused trigger.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks keyboard support for non-buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's `init` function retrieves `_trigger` via selector but does not verify if it is a native button. If it is a `div`, it lacks `tabindex` and `keydown` handlers for Enter/Space, making it inaccessible to keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1520: In `init`, check `if (_trigger.tagName !== 'BUTTON')`. If true, set `tabindex="0"`, `role="button"`, and add a `keydown` listener for Enter/Space to call `toggle()`.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onApply` function in `discountCodeInput` displays an error message in `_statusEl` when a code is invalid but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the `[data-discount-input]` field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid, violating WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2595: In the `if (!result.valid)` block, add `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the `if (result.valid)` block, add `_inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
Cookie consent trigger missing aria-expanded state
Cookie consent actions not announced to screen readers
Theme switcher triggers lack keyboard support for non-buttons
FAQ accordion triggers lack Enter/Space activation for non-buttons
Mobile navigation trigger lacks keyboard support for non-buttons
Discount code input error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and announces the state change, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers unaware that the menu is now expanded, as only the close function sets aria-expanded="false".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function within dropdownMenu removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel and announces the change, but it omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", causing the expanded state to be unreported to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. This prevents assistive technology from immediately identifying the field as invalid upon focus or validation.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open and close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module emits custom events when dropdowns open or close, but it does not write to the shared liveRegion to announce these state changes. Users relying on screen readers are not informed when a dropdown menu becomes visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner does not announce banner opening via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _showBanner function displays the cookie consent dialog and traps focus, but it does not announce the appearance of the banner via the live region. Screen reader users may not realize a new dialog has appeared requiring attention.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent module adds a click listener to [data-cookie-prefs] to show the banner, but it does not initialize aria-expanded or aria-haspopup on this trigger element. Users are not informed that this button controls a popup dialog.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator._animate does not announce counter updates via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function updates the text content of stat counters dynamically as they scroll into view, but it does not announce the final value or the update via a live region. Screen reader users may miss the updated statistics.

fpTitles: statCounterAnimator._animate does not announce counter updates via live region

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the field as invalid programmatically, relying solely on visual cues or description text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function makes the drawer visible via CSS class but does not remove aria-hidden="true" (or set aria-hidden="false"). If the drawer is hidden off-screen via transform/opacity rather than display:none, it remains perceivable to screen readers in its closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1660: function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); ... }

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the drawer via CSS class removal but does not set aria-hidden="true". If the drawer uses off-screen positioning, it remains in the accessibility tree, allowing users to tab into hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1690: function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function updates textContent rapidly during the count-up animation. The element lacks aria-live="polite", so screen readers do not announce the changing values, missing the dynamic content update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5075: animateNumber(0, cfg.target, { duration, easing : easing, onUpdate  : (val) => { el.textContent = _format(val, cfg); }, onComplete: ()    => { el.textContent = _format(cfg.target, cfg); el.setAttribute('aria-label', el.textContent); }, }); // Ensure el has aria-live="polite" in HTML or add: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.fix: Line 4965: qsa(SELECTOR).forEach(el => { el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); _obs.observe(el); });

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle uses aria-checked on native button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function sets aria-checked on the toggle element regardless of its tag. If the element is a native <button>, aria-checked is invalid (buttons should use aria-pressed). This confuses assistive technologies about the widget's state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3185: function _syncToggleState(period) { ... _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { ... }); if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack) { const isYear = period === 'year'; if (_toggleEl.tagName === 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); } else { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)); } ... } }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
