issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` utility function adds the error element to `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field has an error, regardless of the description.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `srUtils.markInvalid`: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after adding the describedby relation.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid()` utility function removes the error description but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` (or remove the attribute) on the field. The field may remain in an invalid state in the accessibility tree even after the user corrects the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `srUtils.markValid`: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in `faqAccordion` emit custom events but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Answer expanded") to the live region. Screen reader users are not notified when an accordion item changes state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `faqAccordion.open`: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters()` function updates the product count badge and visibility of cards but does not announce the change (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to the live region. Users are not informed that the filter has been applied and results updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `productFilter.applyFilters`: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));` after updating the DOM.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod()` function updates price displays and toggle states but does not announce the billing change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to the live region. Users are not informed of the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `pricing.setPeriod`: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action result not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onClick()` handler scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action (e.g., "Scrolled back to top") to the live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden change in scroll position.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `scrollToTop._onClick`: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop);` after scrolling.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent status changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onAccept()` and `_onDecline()` functions hide the banner and update consent state but do not announce the decision (e.g., "Cookie preferences saved") to the live region. Users are not confirmed that their choice was registered.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `cookieConsent._onAccept`: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved);`.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner fade out does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_hideBanner()` function uses `fadeOut()` which sets `opacity: 0` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the banner element during the transition. The banner remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `cookieConsent._hideBanner`: add `setAttr(_bannerEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` before calling `fadeOut`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch panel close on outside click not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onDocumentClick()` handler closes the suggestion panel when clicking outside but does not announce that the panel has closed. Screen reader users may believe the suggestions are still available.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `siteSearch._onDocumentClick`: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.searchClearing);` inside the `_close()` call or directly in the handler.

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter column highlight not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `highlightColumn()` function visually highlights a plan column in the comparison table but does not announce the change to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed which plan is currently being compared or highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `planComparisonHighlighter.highlightColumn`: add `liveRegion.announce(`${planId} plan highlighted`);` after toggling the class.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onChipClick()` handler removes a filter chip and updates the product list but does not announce the removal (e.g., "Filter removed") to the live region. Users are not confirmed that the filter has been cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function `activeFilterChips._onChipClick`: add `liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${key} removed`);` after calling `productFilter.setFilter` or `removeTag`.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised on registration
scrollToTop action result not announced via live region
cookieConsent status changes not announced via live region
siteSearch panel close on outside click not announced
activeFilterChips removal action not announced

issuesFound.title: Form input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in the contact form collects personal information (work email) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from automatically filling the field, reducing efficiency for users with cognitive or motor disabilities (SC 1.3.5).

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing distinguishing label

While some have aria-label attributes, the instance at line 47 ("Main navigation") and line 107 ("Account navigation") and the four in the footer create multiple unlabeled or potentially confusing navigation regions. However, upon re-examination, all nav elements in the document have explicit aria-label attributes: "Main navigation", "Account navigation", "Products navigation", "Resources navigation", "Company navigation", "Legal navigation", and "Social media links". This issue is invalid based on the provided code.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the control accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1302: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant (.btn-sm) has a min-height of 36px, which falls within the 24–43px range and is below the 44px recommended target size. Users with motor impairments may find it difficult to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 524: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: Heading elements use letter-spacing: -0.025em (tight), which reduces the space between characters. Negative letter-spacing can reduce readability for users with low vision or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 175: `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-wrap: balance; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour change on hover to distinguish them from surrounding text. issuesFound.fix: Line 350: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing a high-contrast alternative such as a solid border. Users in forced-colors mode lose focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 361-364: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator for dropdown menu links without providing a visible alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, overriding the box-shadow defined in #search-input:focus and leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 565: `#search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible class only adjusts outline-offset but does not restore the outline style removed by the base :focus-visible rule, resulting in no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 377: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible class only sets outline-color but does not restore the outline style removed by the base :focus-visible rule, resulting in no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 381: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This makes the button difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 572: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links

This prevents screen-reader-only text from being announced correctly.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible and does not hide it from layout, contrary to its purpose. It fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 454), causing spatial movement on state changes without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add inside existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block or create new: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transition on transform (line 1024) and transform properties that cause spatial movement (rotateY, rotateX), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 1033-1038 is empty and does not disable the transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition (line 1002) and has a hover transform (line 1008), with no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Add: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on feature cards
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card selector includes transform in its transition (line 1094), causing movement on hover. The existing @media block at line 1104 only overrides the transform value on hover but does not remove the transition, so animation still occurs.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on stat items
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item selector includes transform in its transition (line 1221). The existing @media block at line 1229 only sets transform: none on hover but does not disable the transition, allowing animation to continue.
issuesFound.fix: Add to line 1230 inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): .stat-item { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on pricing cards

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without any prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the spatial movement.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.fix: Line 558: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-full); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); appearance: none; -webkit-appearance: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, creating a touch target below the 44px recommended size and the 24px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 567: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making them difficult for users with motor impairments to select accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 20px; height: 20px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which can cause vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion when buttons are hovered or focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 428: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` (remove transform from transition)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button includes transform in its transition without a reduced motion override, potentially triggering vestibular disorders when the button state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); min-height: 32px; border-radius: var(--radius-xl); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` (remove transform)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by making words run together visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 183: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or set to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use color #94a3b8 against background #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and makes links difficult to read for low vision users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1152: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social media links

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer copyright links

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social media links

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 354: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector again removes the outline without replacement. Users in Windows High Contrast mode rely on the outline to see focus; removing it creates a critical barrier.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class only adjusts outline-offset to -3px but inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. No visible focus ring is rendered, leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 377: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Contact form first name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form first name input collects personal data (given name) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from automatically filling the field, reducing usability for users with cognitive disabilities or motor impairments who rely on autofill functionality.
issuesFound.fix: Line where contactFirstName is defined: No change needed to selector. In contactForm.init, after selecting the field, add: `contactFirstName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form last name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form last name input collects personal data (family name) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents automatic filling by browsers and assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In contactForm.init, after selecting the field, add: `contactLastName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email input collects personal data (email address) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents automatic filling by browsers and assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In contactForm.init, after selecting the field, add: `contactEmail.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form company input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form company input collects personal data (organization) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents automatic filling by browsers and assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In contactForm.init, after selecting the field, add: `contactCompany.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'organization');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input collects personal data (email address) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents automatic filling by browsers and assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In newsletterForm.init, after selecting the field, add: `newsletterEmail.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard full name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard's full name input (wizard_name) collects personal data (full name) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents automatic filling by browsers and assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In multiStepWizard.init, when binding fields, for the wizard_name field add: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard email input missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard card name input missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer implements a focus trap and closes when clicking outside, indicating it is a modal dialog. However, it lacks aria-modal="true", which is required to inform assistive technologies that the content underneath is inert while the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line in mobileNav.open after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` Line in mobileNav.close before closing: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal');`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attributes on personal data inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields (#contact-first-name, #contact-last-name, #contact-email) and newsletter email field (#newsletter-email) clearly collect personal data based on their IDs and names, but the JavaScript initialization code does not programmatically add the HTML autocomplete attribute (e.g., autocomplete="given-name", autocomplete="family-name", autocomplete="email") to these inputs. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from correctly identifying the purpose of these fields, failing WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750 (newsletterForm.init): Add `newsletterEmail.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` after selecting the element. Line 2800 (contactForm.init): Add `contactFirstName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');`, `contactLastName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');`, `contactEmail.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`, and `contactCompany.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'organization');` after selecting the respective elements.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications incorrectly use role="alert" for all message types
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager.createElement call assigns role="alert" to all toast types (success, error, warning, info). The alert role implies aria-live="assertive" and interrupts screen reader users immediately. For non-error messages (success, info), this is overly aggressive and can disorient users. Success and info toasts should use role="status" (polite) while error toasts may use role="alert" (assertive).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150 (toast creation): Change `role: 'alert'` to `role: type === 'error' ? 'alert' : 'status'` so that error toasts remain as alerts and all other types use the polite status role.

fpTitles: Missing autocomplete attributes on personal data inputs
Toast notifications incorrectly use role="alert" for all message types

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are opened or closed via click, keyboard, or hover, the functions update the aria-expanded attribute and toggle CSS classes but do not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that the menu has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1338: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`
    Line 1358: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hidden state not conveyed to accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the sticky header is hidden via the `hide()` function, it only toggles a CSS class. Interactive elements within the header remain in the accessibility tree and can be focused by keyboard users even when the header is visually hidden, causing focus to be lost or invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1687: Add `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden);`
    Line 1695: Add `removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');` after `removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden);`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips focus lost on chip removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip remove button is activated, the chip element is removed from the DOM without transferring focus to another element. Keyboard users lose focus context and focus moves to the document body.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2680: Before `removeElement(chip)`, add focus management:
    `const container = _containerEl; const prev = chip.previousElementSibling; (prev ? prev.querySelector('button') : container).focus();`

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField focus lost on file removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a file entry remove button is clicked, the row is removed from the DOM but focus is not moved to another element. Focus is lost to the document body.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2844: Before `removeElement(entry.rowEl)`, add:
    `const list = binding.listEl; const prev = entry.rowEl.previousElementSibling; (prev ? prev.querySelector('button') : list).focus();`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause state not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button toggles automatic slide rotation and updates aria-pressed, but does not announce the state change (playing or paused) to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3118: Add `liveRegion.announce(_playing ? STRINGS.carouselPlaying || 'Automatic rotation started' : STRINGS.carouselPaused || 'Automatic rotation paused');`

issuesFound.title: Navigation keyboard shortcuts conflict with assistive technologies
issuesFound.explanation: Keyboard shortcuts using Alt+S, Alt+N, Alt+M, Alt+F, and Alt+T may conflict with screen reader shortcuts (e.g., Alt+S is commonly used by NVDA for Settings). This prevents screen reader users from accessing their native functions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1765: Add a configuration option to disable these shortcuts or change the modifier key from Alt to Ctrl+Alt, and document the shortcuts in an accessible help section.

issuesFound.title: productFilter focus lost when focused card is filtered out
issuesFound.explanation: If a user has keyboard focus on a product card that gets hidden by a filter operation, focus is lost when the card is hidden. The code does not check if the active element is being hidden before removing it from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2535: In filterAnimation.run, before hiding elements, add:
    `const active = document.activeElement; if (active && toHideEls.includes(active)) { toShowEls[0]?.focus() || _gridEl.focus(); }`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent focus not returned after closing banner
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is closed, the focus trap is deactivated but focus is not returned to the element that triggered the banner or to the main content. Focus may be lost to the document body.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3543: Store the previously focused element before showing the banner (`const trigger = document.activeElement;`), and in _hideBanner, add `trigger?.focus() || qs('main')?.focus();` before `fadeOut`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.auditImages incorrectly marks informative images as decorative
issuesFound.explanation: The function finds images missing alt attributes and sets `alt=""` on them, assuming they are decorative. If the HTML contains informative images without alt text, this incorrectly hides them from screen readers instead of flagging them for content authors to fix.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3290: Instead of setting `alt=""`, add a visible error class or console error, and do not modify the alt attribute: `warn('Image missing alt:', img); img.classList.add('missing-alt');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing modal dialog semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer traps focus and obscures the main content but does not have `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal="true"`, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand they are in a modal context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1283: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'role', 'dialog');` and `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` when opening the drawer.
    Line 1290: Remove these attributes when closing.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2350: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult missing aria-invalid update
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function calls markInvalid but does not ensure aria-invalid is set to "true" on the field, and the valid case does not explicitly clear it (though markValid does).
issuesFound.fix: Line 6115: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the else block (invalid case) after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2855: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` at the end of _openInstance. Line 2890: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` at the end of _closeInstance before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, keyboard focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, potentially stranding keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function reveals the mobile navigation drawer and sets internal state, but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu has expanded, even though the close() function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function manages CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() function expands the answer panel and updates internal state, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing that the panel is expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion close() function collapses the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility tree in an inconsistent state where the panel is hidden but the trigger still indicates it is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2825: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() omits aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The site search _open() function displays the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the input element, violating the ARIA combobox pattern and preventing screen readers from announcing that suggestions are available.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function associates an error message with a form field via aria-describedby, but neglects to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true". Consequently, screen readers do not announce the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1603: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer visually by removing CSS classes, but if the drawer is positioned off-screen via CSS (transform: translateX) rather than display:none, it remains in the accessibility tree. The function does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` and insert `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` in the open() function after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function initializes the mobile menu but fails to activate the focus trap (_trap), allowing keyboard focus to escape the drawer and traverse the background content. The close() function deactivates the trap, but it is never activated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1125: Insert `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` after the line `_lockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to move focus to first menu item
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the first focusable element within the drawer, forcing keyboard users to tab through the entire page to reach the menu content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: Insert `const first = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (first) first.focus();` after activating the focus trap.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is injected into the DOM and made visible when the user scrolls, but it lacks an aria-label or text content, providing no accessible name for screen reader users to identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: Insert `_btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');` immediately after the line `_initialised = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by removing or adding the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but fails to synchronize the aria-expanded state on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdowns.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is opened, the function animates the panel height and adds CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", this asymmetry leaves opened items incorrectly announced as collapsed.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons that remain pressed when active, but the code only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid or valid, the code updates aria-describedby to link error messages but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clears it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify which fields have failed validation.

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters and the product grid updates to show or hide cards, no announcement is made to screen readers regarding the number of results or that the filter has been applied. Users are unaware that the content has changed.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, prices update visually with animation, but no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the billing period change or the new prices.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion expand and collapse actions are not announced to screen readers despite defined announcement strings (STRINGS.faqOpened and STRINGS.faqClosed). Users receive no feedback when accordion items open or close.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden is not set to "true". Screen readers may still access the visually hidden menu content. Conversely, when opened, aria-hidden is not removed.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button only handles click events. If the element referenced by SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn is not a native button element (e.g., a div or link with role="button"), keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space keys.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion panel is closed on page load, violating the ARIA combobox pattern requirements for initial state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The code declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). When the menu opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab to background content behind the modal overlay.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu outside click loses focus
issuesFound.explanation: When clicking outside to close dropdown menus, the _closeAll() function is called without returning focus to the trigger button. Keyboard focus is lost to the document body, forcing users to navigate back to the menu from the beginning of the page.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (_clearBtn) only handles click events. If the element is not a native button, keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space to clear the search field.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled by a button stored in `_trigger`, but the `open` and `close` functions only manipulate CSS classes on the drawer and body elements. They never call `setAttribute('aria-expanded', ...)` on the trigger button, so screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the `open` function animates the panel and updates state, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the item's trigger button. Although the `close` function sets `aria-expanded="false"`, the missing "true" value in the open state leaves the accessibility tree inconsistent.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via these helper functions, but neither function updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger element to reflect the visibility state of the associated panel.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab bar uses buttons that behave as toggle buttons (one is always pressed), but the code only toggles a CSS class. It never sets `aria-pressed` to indicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Screen readers rely on this attribute to convey the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it selects the trigger button but does not set the initial `aria-expanded` state to reflect that the menu is closed on page load.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that expands/collapses a suggestion listbox. While the `show` and `hide` functions toggle `aria-expanded`, the initialisation code never sets the attribute to `false` on page load, leaving the state undefined until the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realise the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close do not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ answer updates the visual content and the URL hash, but no live-region announcement informs screen reader users that the content has changed or which question is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear function does not announce search cleared
issuesFound.explanation: When the user activates the clear button, the search input is emptied and the suggestion panel closes, but no announcement confirms that the search has been cleared. This leaves screen reader users uncertain whether the action succeeded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not remove drawer from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, the drawer is visually hidden via CSS class removal (likely moving it off-screen), but the code does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element. Screen readers may still be able to navigate to the off-screen content.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button not initialised with aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button's `aria-pressed` state is only updated when the `play()` or `pause()` functions are called. If autoplay is disabled, the button starts in the "paused" state but `aria-pressed="false"` is never explicitly set, leaving the attribute undefined.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu hover timer does not clear on destroy
issuesFound.explanation: When the dropdown menu module is destroyed, any pending hover timers (inst.hoverTimer) are not cleared. This can cause memory leaks or attempt to access removed DOM elements if the user hovers out after destruction.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via the open() and close() functions, which add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer. However, neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger) to reflect the expanded or collapsed state. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170 (open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`. Line 2200 (close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, which manipulate the hidden attribute and CSS classes. These functions do not set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger button (inst.trigger), violating the ARIA disclosure pattern and leaving screen reader users unaware of the panel's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2350 (_openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 2370 (_closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the start of the function before the early return check.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search suggestion panel is toggled via _open() and _close() functions. While the input element has aria-controls pointing to the suggestion list, the aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect whether the suggestions are visible or hidden. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state of the combobox popup.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100 (_open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 3120 (_close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation detects an invalid field, the markInvalid helper adds the error element to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Assistive technologies will not identify the field as having invalid entry, preventing users from discovering the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid helper removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The field remains programmatically marked as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1660: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function after clearing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters to the product grid, the visible card count changes and results are updated dynamically. The applyFilters function emits a custom event but does not write to an aria-live region to announce the result count or filter change to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550 (inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion expands and collapses answer panels when users click question buttons. Although the open and close functions accept an announceToSR parameter, they never call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the panel has expanded or collapsed, nor do they use the parameter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4200 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : 'Answer expanded');` before the emit call. Line 4230 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : 'Answer collapsed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen reader users. Users navigating with keyboards or screen readers will not be informed that a submenu has appeared or disappeared, making it difficult to locate the new content or confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2350 (_openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after opening the panel. Line 2370 (_closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` at the start of the function before closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly, but the open and close functions do not announce these state changes via the live region. Screen reader users will not know that the navigation menu has opened or that focus has moved into a new context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` after `_lockScroll();`. Line 2200 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after `_unlockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the displayed prices across all plan cards. The setPeriod function emits a custom event but does not announce the billing period change or price updates to screen reader users, who may not realize the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3800: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it remains in the DOM and is likely hidden using CSS transforms or off-screen positioning rather than display:none. The close function does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element (_drawer), meaning screen readers may still be able to navigate to the hidden navigation content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200 (close): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line 2170 (open): Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function locates the trigger button (_trigger) but does not initialise the aria-expanded attribute to "false" when the page loads. Since the navigation starts in a closed state (_open = false), the trigger must explicitly declare this state to ensure assistive technologies recognize the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250 (init, after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` to establish the relationship and initial state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are registered, the trigger buttons have aria-haspopup and aria-controls set, but aria-expanded is never initialised to "false" for closed dropdowns. Screen readers will not recognize the initial collapsed state of the disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2320 (register, after setting aria-controls): Add `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch init function sets up the search input with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but it does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the input element. Since the suggestion panel starts closed (_searchState.open = false), the combobox input must declare this initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150 (init, after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable but never initialises it with createFocusTrap or activates it. When the navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab out of the modal context and navigate to background content. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170 (open): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`. Line 2200 (close): Change `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();` to ensure _trap is created before checking isActive, or simply call `_trap && _trap.deactivate();` after ensuring _trap is assigned in open.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view toggle missing aria-pressed initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter init function sets up view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) and updates their aria-pressed state when clicked, but it does not initialise aria-pressed on page load to reflect the default view mode stored in _productFilterState.viewMode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3600 (init, after restoring saved view mode): Add `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden by adding or removing the hidden attribute, but the associated trigger button's aria-expanded state is never synchronized. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line: In dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of the function before the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open and _close do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element functions as a combobox that expands and collapses a suggestion listbox, but the code never toggles aria-expanded on the input to communicate this state change. Screen reader users are unaware when the suggestion list appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In siteSearch._open: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the end of the function. Line: In siteSearch._close: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of the function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons that remain pressed when active, but the code only toggles CSS classes and does not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active because the pressed state is not communicated programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In productFilter._syncTabAriaStates: Replace the existing loop body with: `const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When field validation fails, the validator updates CSS classes and aria-describedby references, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input. When validation passes, it does not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In inlineValidator._renderResult: In the valid block, add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);`. In the invalid block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded or aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation initializes, the trigger button lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute and the aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer panel. This prevents screen readers from understanding the initial collapsed state and the relationship between the trigger and the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.init: After `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` and `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown components are registered, the trigger button lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute, incorrectly implying an expanded state by default until the user interacts with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In dropdownMenu.register: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute when the component initializes, failing to indicate that the suggestion listbox is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In siteSearch.init: After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu opens or closes, no announcement is made via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu state has changed and that new content is available or has been removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.open: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` at the end of the function. Line: In mobileNav.close: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus visually reveals or hides content, but screen reader users receive no confirmation via the live region that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` at the end. Line: In dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` at the end.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse with animation when toggled, but the state change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware whether their action to expand or collapse an item succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In faqAccordion.open: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title));` at the end of the function. Line: In faqAccordion.close: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title));` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually across all plan cards, but screen reader users are not informed that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In pricing.setPeriod: After updating the plan cards, add: `const msg = period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly; liveRegion.announce(msg);`.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar lacks role="progressbar"
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar element receives aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax attributes, but lacks role="progressbar", causing screen readers to not recognize it as a progress indicator and potentially ignore the value updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In multiStepWizard._updateProgress: At the start of the function, add: `if (!_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) setAttr(_progressBar, 'role', 'progressbar');`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button's aria-pressed state is only updated when the button is toggled, but not initialized when the carousel first loads. If autoplay starts automatically, the button should indicate it is pressed (playing) from the start.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In testimonialsCarousel.init: After `if (_playBtn) {`, add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing);` to synchronize the initial state.

fpTitles: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
multiStepWizard progress bar lacks role="progressbar"
testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer trigger button does not have its aria-expanded attribute set to "true" when the menu opens. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not set aria-expanded="false" when their associated panel is closed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState() missing aria-pressed update

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation menu changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the live region announcer.
issuesFound.fix: Line: emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); Add after: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation menu does not announce the state change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer }); Add after: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but does not announce the menu state change to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel }); Add after: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel }); Add after: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ answer panel changes visible content without announcing the expansion to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Add after: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title) || `${item.title} answer expanded`);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Applying product filters updates the visible product grid and count but does not announce the filter results or current filter category to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', { count: totalMatches, state: Object.assign({}, s) }); Add after: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || `Showing ${s.activeCategory} products`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates price displays across all plan cards but does not announce the billing period change to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button changes the page scroll position but does not announce the action completion to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips._onChipClick() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Removing a filter chip via click updates the product grid but does not announce the filter removal to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() missing aria-invalid setting
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation errors are not communicated to assistive technologies via aria-invalid. The function marks fields visually and links error messages but omits the aria-invalid attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); Add after: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); AND Line: srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); Add after: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" on page load when the menu is closed by default.

issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" when the menu is registered and hidden by default.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init() missing aria-expanded initialisation

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component receives children instead of message prop in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a `message` prop to render text, but NewsletterForm passes the error text as children. This causes the component to render nothing because `message` is undefined, preventing screen-reader users from hearing the error announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1638: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." role="alert" />

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component receives children instead of message prop in ContactForm
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a `message` prop, but ContactForm passes the error text as children. Since the component checks `if (!message) return null`, the error remains invisible to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1778: <ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." role="alert" />

This broken ARIA reference means screen readers cannot associate the error message with the input field when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1704: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for contact-email has aria-describedby="contact-email-error" (line 1730), but the parent FormGroup is not passed errorId="contact-email-error" (line 1719). This creates a broken ARIA reference that prevents assistive technology from reading the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1719: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for contact-subject has aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" (line 1746), but the parent FormGroup is not passed errorId="contact-subject-error" (line 1735). This broken reference means the error message is not programmatically associated with the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea references non-existent error element

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has misplaced aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the outer overlay div instead of the dialog div. According to ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be placed on the element with role="dialog" to correctly indicate modality to assistive technologies. When placed on the overlay, screen readers may not properly constrain navigation to the modal content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> Line 2060: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: StatItem paragraph with invalid aria-label
issuesFound.explanation:
The stat value paragraph uses aria-label to provide a screen-reader-friendly version of the value (e.g., "40,000 plus"), but aria-label is not supported on

elements because they have no implicit role that accepts a name. Screen readers will ignore the aria-label and may announce only the visual text (e.g., "40,000+").
issuesFound.fix:

{stat.valueLabel}{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Reporting" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The Reporting category filter button lacks aria-pressed, preventing screen reader users from knowing it is the active filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1560: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'reporting'} to the specific button instance.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Each pricing plan card is an article element but lacks aria-labelledby to reference its heading. Screen reader users navigating by landmark will hear "article" without context for which plan it is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1724: Add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article element.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation uses a nav element but lacks an aria-label. With multiple nav elements on the page (footer, social, skip links), screen reader users cannot distinguish the purpose of this navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: Add aria-label="Main" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation inside the mobile menu panel uses a nav element without an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions when using screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: Add aria-label="Mobile main" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not set aria-busy. Screen reader users are not notified when the button enters a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 935: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link (Products) has a visual indicator via CSS but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1169: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation has a visual indicator but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing it as the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section uses a section element but lacks aria-labelledby to reference its visible h1 heading. Screen reader users navigating by landmark will hear only "region" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar uses a section element but lacks aria-labelledby to reference its visible h2 heading, leaving screen reader users without context for the region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for the marquee visual effect lacks aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1474: Add aria-hidden="true" to the second ul element (the clone).

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container uses a nav element but lacks an aria-label. With multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this as the skip navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 826: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: StarRating widget missing role
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating container has an aria-label describing the rating but lacks role="img", so screen readers may not treat it as a single image representation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 892: Add role="img" to the outer span element.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar stat value missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value "40,000+" is rendered without aria-label, so screen readers may not read it as "40,000 plus" as intended. The valueLabel data property is provided but not used.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1396: Add aria-label={stat.valueLabel} to the p element with className="stat-value".

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide 1 not hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive slides remain in the DOM without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to read all testimonials instead of only the visible one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the div with role="group" and aria-roledescription="slide".

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide 2 not hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive slides remain in the DOM without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to read all testimonials instead of only the visible one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the div for the second testimonial.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide 3 not hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive slides remain in the DOM without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to read all testimonials instead of only the visible one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the div for the third testimonial.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide 4 not hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive slides remain in the DOM without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to read all testimonials instead of only the visible one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the div for the fourth testimonial.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div. This can cause screen readers to treat the overlay as the modal container instead of the dialog content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1943: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay div and add it to the inner div with role="dialog" (line 1945).
